title: Slice DAG artifact — to-slices output / slice-conductor input

source.description: plan file path, feature description, or existing Linear parent id this DAG was decomposed from

linear_parent_id.description: null in dry-run; filled by to-slices publish mode after DAG approval

approved.description: the owner approved this DAG. The conductor REFUSES to dispatch any agent or perform any Linear write unless true.

concurrency_ceiling.description: HARD CAP on simultaneous teammates (≤5, per the agent-teams docs' 3-5 guidance). The DAG owns parallelism STRUCTURE; this only bounds the launch set = ready-frontier ∩ ceiling.

id.description: Stable slice id (e.g. S1), distinct from linear_issue_id which is assigned at publish.

touch_set.description: Explicit file/glob paths this slice may modify. The conductor's runtime boundary check asserts the committed diff ⊆ this set.

blocked_by.description: Slice ids that must merge before this one starts. Carries BOTH semantic dependencies and shared-file collision edges. Must reference existing slice ids and form a DAG (Kahn-validated).

layer_rank.description: ADVISORY only (optional). Vertical slices span layers, so this is not a hard gate — validate_dag derives the effective max rank from touch_set for display and relies on Kahn acyclicity for cycle prevention. Layers come from the project-config `layers` list; a rough generic map: 0=pure-core 1=domain-models 2=config 3=interfaces/ports 4=adapters 5=app-core 6=entrypoint.

shared_core.description: Touches a serialize-always file (models.ts, interfaces.ts, core/, config.ts, mapping.ts, package.json, lockfile, .secrets.baseline). DERIVED by validate_dag from touch_set — the declared flag must agree or the gate fails. Two concurrent shared_core slices are a collision (foundational files run serially).

maintenance.description: Trivial/maintenance slice → the conductor SKIPS the local `greptile review` shift-left in the per-slice light gate (loop.md §5a); in-team review + the AC gate + the boundary check still run. It still merges into the campaign integration branch like every slice — the WHOLE branch ships to main via ONE babysit-pr (there is no per-slice PR).

linear_issue_id.description: null in dry-run; the ISSUE-### id filled by to-slices publish mode.
